item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to be honest. I first read this as Mr.Strips.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just read all of the Little Misters entries and I gotta say, I've never been so fully captivated by a series of SCP's before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, there should be one for all but Redd. That would keep a mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, both work for me. Hell, it wouldn't be hard to envision them co-existing — Troy's Stripes is creepy as hell, and the Sweetie entry establishes that Misters can have multiple variations, sometimes even as the same person, FWIW.

Also, read in conjunction with Miss Heir, this goes from good to great. It also makes it even easier to reconcile the two versions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm, could be. I'm upvoting now for the thing people pointed out about the redactions, goshdarnit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm. I prefer this version of Mr. Stripes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've actually used this before, I was just confused if he had or if I was talking to someone else entirely. I told somebody before I changed mine (although he could have done this too); his change was just very sudden.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like they changed their username. Wikidot allows you to do that a limited number of times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also as a side note: didn't your name used to be Live2Die? Cause if not last night I was tripping some serious balls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I was reading this I had a reflexive huff at 'Containment procedures are blackbarred', until it dawned on me what Mr Stripes did.

Very clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fun! And I want more misters. I want the set filled out. Only then can I truly rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't decide whether I'm amused or annoyed that any redaction in this file could be part of the object's effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely another great example of telling an interesting story with an SCP article. +1 TG. Well done. Stands on its own for sure.

item.title: plus one for fun
item.content:encoded: Super cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am actually choosing my words pretty poorly tonight. It's not that I hate them, it's just that if you take them out of their context, what are they really? For example, if I'm a newcomer to the site, and this is the first thing I read, I'm going to be pretty confused. It lacks the back story (instead provided by other articles like it) that other skips work very hard to build in themselves. And don't get me wrong, I love wondertainment, just not this particular splinter of it. The reason of my posting on this one in particular is I feel it is the least interesting and narrative giving one to date. Think about it. Take 1048, another wondertainmnt object. It manages to include a new, interesting concept, provide interesting exposition about said concept, and wrap itself up with a satisfying conclusion. I feel this article is simply a bridge, as to me it only includes a slight amount of exposition. No true beginning, no real end, it is just making way for future works. And that's fine and all, but I dont necessarily think that belongs on the mainlist.

TL;DR: I see nothing wrong w/ the page itself, (although it's not the best, for the reasons mentioned) I just don't think it should be man list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would think continuing it here wouldn't be a bad idea, since this pertains to the article in question.
I don't understand why you hate the little misters. Yes, they're a group of people that have anomalous effects, but they're also Wondertainment product that all relate to each other in a special way. I dunno, maybe I'm just a sucker for Wondertainment, but I find this idea really cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This definitely stands on its own. I don't really understand the complaints about this. Is it because… it references other Misters? I really can't understand the logic here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's odd. I feel this is actually one of the least standalone of the entire series. I'm mainly referring to the addendum and the interview logs. Anyway, I see nothing wrong with linking an article on anothers work, but that this particular line is no longer innovating. It seems as though instead of creating new and interesting ideas, it's just a bunch of humanoids (I'll admit, there are some exceptions) with certain basic and uninteresting effects. This may just be my opinion, but these belong in a more canonical format. The little men series is an incredibly loosely organized and hard to follow thing spread through SCP and Tale, and I think just think it should stick to one.

EDIT: I do not harbor some kind of deep hatred for just this page, but the series itself. Perhaps I should create a forum post instead of continuing this here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, if he really was the first Little Mister, it might mean that Mr. Forgetful was just an "upgraded" version of Mr. Stripes. It might be why Stripes was the first one sent to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure why he needs to be the first one we contained (I get that he's not actually the first one created; I like the stuff with Mr. Lie), but I've always wondered what Mr. Stripes would do, and this is a neat interpretation of his name. That said, he kind of feels like an underpowered Mr. Forgetful variant.

I'm still glad people haven't forgotten the Little Misters, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, reiterating on my pervious post, I do believe that this would stand on its own, since it's an item with an interesting history, lots of potential for tales, and says a lot about Wondertainment. I seriously don't get why people say it wouldn't be able to stand on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I believe that this would have been able to stand up on its own. Anyways, what's wrong with having an SCP based off of some others? Many authors link some/all of their SCPs or SCPs of another person, and I don't see how this is any different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing Chork's sentiments. This one wouldn't really stand on its own. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can never have to much wondertainment. Except you can. Maybe it's just me, but I feel the effect of these is not as captivating as before. And it makes me sad.

Ok, please ignore the broad and generally unhelpful statement above, and simply answer this question: without the other little men, how could this stand up on its own? This article just further emphasizes why I think these should all be a tale series, and not necessarily on the main list. Imagine an article like this with no others to back it up. It wouldn't survive a few days. It's like banking on a 682 cross test to save your article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ My guess is that
- Could it be?

Mr. Stripes is Mr. Lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, first and foremost, thank you for getting more Little Misters up and running. Second:

Spoilers?
How close was I?

Stripes is under Lie's influence, which makes him lie, meaning that Mr. Lie is the only Little Mister that Mr. Stripes has seen, and also possibly meaning that Stripes was the last one created.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So here we are! Spawned from my desire to continue Miss Heir, we have my interpretation of Mr. Stripes and Mr. Lie!

Many thanks to Decibelle, who I annoyed about this endlessly both as a draft and in the brainstorming phase. Thanks to TroyL and Crayne for helping me tighten up the draft, and the Live2Die, JamJam66, DrLeanard, BobDragonslayer, and honestly a bunch of people because I was really neurotic about uploading this.

Edit: Look what Sunny drew! :3
